tasks.title: Add 4 new columns + run_status index to accommodation_external_reputation Drizzle schema
tasks.description:
Modify `packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation-external/accommodation_external_reputation.dbschema.ts` to:
1. Import `ExternalReputationRunStatusPgEnum` from `../enums.dbschema.ts`.
2. Add columns inside the table definition:

3. Add an index on `run_status` in the table's index config object:
   `accommodation_external_reputation_runStatus_idx: index('accommodation_external_reputation_runStatus_idx').on(table.runStatus)`

Key file:

Acceptance criteria:

- The four new columns are present in the TypeScript type inferred by Drizzle.
- No existing columns are altered or removed.

tasks.title: Generate Drizzle migration for new enum + columns and commit migration file
tasks.description:
Run `pnpm --filter @repo/db db:generate` to produce the versioned migration SQL file that creates the `external_reputation_run_status_enum` pg enum and adds the 4 new columns + index to `accommodation_external_reputation`.

Steps:
1. `pnpm --filter @repo/db db:generate` — generates a new migration file in `packages/db/src/migrations/`.
2. Open the generated SQL and verify it contains:
   - `CREATE TYPE external_reputation_run_status_enum AS ENUM ('idle', 'pending', 'running');`
   - `ALTER TABLE accommodation_external_reputation ADD COLUMN run_status ...`
   - `ALTER TABLE accommodation_external_reputation ADD COLUMN apify_run_id ...`
   - `ALTER TABLE accommodation_external_reputation ADD COLUMN apify_dataset_id ...`
   - `ALTER TABLE accommodation_external_reputation ADD COLUMN run_started_at ...`
   - `CREATE INDEX accommodation_external_reputation_runStatus_idx ...`
3. Commit the generated migration file.
4. `pnpm --filter @repo/db db:migrate` locally to verify it applies cleanly.

Key files:
- packages/db/src/migrations/<timestamp>_<name>.sql (new, generated)
- packages/db/src/migrations/meta/ (snapshot files, auto-updated)

Acceptance criteria:
- Migration file is committed and passes `pnpm --filter @repo/db typecheck`.
- The schema drift guard (`scripts/check-schema-drift.sh` or CI equivalent) passes — no uncommitted schema changes.

tasks.title: Register HOSPEDA_EXTREP_POLL_SCHEDULE and HOSPEDA_EXTREP_APIFY_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS in env registry
tasks.description:
Add two new env vars to the canonical registry and consuming app.

1. In `packages/config/src/env-registry.*.ts`, register:
   - `HOSPEDA_EXTREP_POLL_SCHEDULE` (string, not secret, default `'*/2 * * * *'`, category 'cron', apps: ['api'], description: 'Cron schedule for the Apify reputation polling job')
   - `HOSPEDA_EXTREP_APIFY_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS` (string/integer, not secret, default `'600000'`, category 'external', apps: ['api'], description: 'Max age of a pending Apify run before the poller marks it as timed out (ms)')

2. Add Zod validation in `apps/api/src/utils/env.ts` (ApiEnvBaseSchema):
   - `HOSPEDA_EXTREP_POLL_SCHEDULE: z.string().default('*/2 * * * *')`
   - `HOSPEDA_EXTREP_APIFY_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS: z.string().default('600000')`

3. Update `apps/api/.env.example` with safe placeholder values.

Key files:
- packages/config/src/env-registry.*.ts
- apps/api/src/utils/env.ts
- apps/api/.env.example

Acceptance criteria:

- `pnpm --filter apps/api typecheck` passes.

tasks.title: Implement startApifyRun in apify-client.ts
Implement getApifyRunStatus in apify-client.ts
Implement getApifyDatasetItems in apify-client.ts
tasks.description:
Add `getApifyDatasetItems` to `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/adapters/apify-client.ts`.

```ts
export async function getApifyDatasetItems(input: {
    token: string;
    datasetId: string;
}): Promise<unknown[]>
```

Implementation details:
- GET `https://api.apify.com/v2/datasets/{datasetId}/items` with `Authorization: Bearer {token}`.
- On 200: return the parsed JSON array.
- On any non-2xx, fetch error, or non-array response: return `[]`. Never throws.

Key file:
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/adapters/apify-client.ts

Acceptance criteria:
- `getApifyDatasetItems` is exported.
- Returns `[]` on failure (not null, not throw).
- `pnpm --filter @repo/service-core typecheck` passes.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for all three async Apify client functions
tasks.description:
Create (or extend) the test file for `apify-client.ts`, covering `startApifyRun`, `getApifyRunStatus`, and `getApifyDatasetItems`.

Test location: `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/adapters/test/apify-client.test.ts` (or matching existing test directory).

Test cases per function:

`startApifyRun`:
- 201 happy path returns `{ runId, defaultDatasetId }`.
- Non-201 (400, 500) returns null.
- Fetch network error returns null.
- Response body missing `data.id` returns null.
- Response body missing `data.defaultDatasetId` returns null.

`getApifyRunStatus`:
- 200 with `SUCCEEDED` status returns correct shape.
- 200 with `RUNNING` status returns correct shape.
- Non-200 returns null.
- Fetch error returns null.
- Missing `data.status` returns null.

`getApifyDatasetItems`:
- 200 with array body returns the array.
- 200 with non-array body returns `[]`.
- Non-200 returns `[]`.
- Fetch error returns `[]`.

All tests mock `fetch` (e.g. via vitest `vi.spyOn(global, 'fetch')` or `vi.fn()`).

Acceptance criteria:
- All test cases pass.
- Coverage on the three new functions >= 90%.

tasks.title: Add startRun + mapDatasetItems optional methods to ReputationAdapter interface
tasks.description:
Modify `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-external-reputation/adapters/adapter.types.ts` to add two optional methods:

```ts
/**
 * Phase A: enqueue an Apify actor run for this listing.
 * Implemented ONLY by adapters that use Apify (Booking fallback, Airbnb).
 * Returns the run ID + dataset ID on success, or null on failure.
 */
startRun?(listing: AccommodationExternalListing): Promise<{ runId: string; datasetId: string } | null>;

/**
 * Phase B: map raw dataset items from a completed Apify run to a reputation result.
 * Pure function — no HTTP calls. Called by the poller after getApifyDatasetItems().
 */
mapDatasetItems?(items: unknown[], listing: AccommodationExternalListing): ReputationFetchResult;
```

Both methods are optional (`?:`) — adapters not using Apify (Google, Generic) do not implement them.

Key file:
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-external-reputation/adapters/adapter.types.ts

Acceptance criteria:
- Interface compiles with both methods as optional.
- Existing Google/Generic adapter implementations continue to satisfy the interface (no new required methods).
- `pnpm --filter @repo/service-core typecheck` passes.

tasks.title: Refactor BookingReputationAdapter: retain JSON-LD fetch, add startRun + mapDatasetItems
tasks.description:
Modify `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-external-reputation/adapters/booking-reputation.adapter.ts`:

1. `fetch(listing)` — UNCHANGED for the JSON-LD fast path. Keep: try `safeExternalFetch` + JSON-LD aggregate rating parse. If JSON-LD succeeds, return result as before (inline, no Apify). If JSON-LD fails/returns all-null aggregates, return the all-null result (the service will decide to go async based on null aggregates + presence of `startRun`).
   - Remove the old `runApifyActor()` fallback call from `fetch()`.

2. Add `startRun(listing)`: call `startApifyRun({ token: creds.apifyToken, actor: creds.apifyBookingActor, actorInput: <same input shape as the existing sync call> })`. Return `{ runId: result.runId, datasetId: result.defaultDatasetId }` or null.

3. Add `mapDatasetItems(items, listing)`: pure mapping from Booking actor output items to `ReputationFetchResult`. Extract `rating` and `reviewsCount` from the actor output shape (verify from SPEC-237 implementation — use same field paths already used in the existing Booking actor parsing logic). Return `ReputationFetchResult`.

Key file:
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-external-reputation/adapters/booking-reputation.adapter.ts

Acceptance criteria:
- `runApifyActor` is NO LONGER called from `fetch()` in this adapter.
- `startRun` and `mapDatasetItems` are implemented and satisfy the updated `ReputationAdapter` interface.
- `pnpm --filter @repo/service-core typecheck` passes.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for BookingReputationAdapter
tasks.description:
Create or extend `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-external-reputation/adapters/test/booking-reputation.adapter.test.ts`.

Test cases:
1. `fetch()` — JSON-LD success path: mock `safeExternalFetch` to return HTML with JSON-LD aggregate rating. Expect non-null `rating` returned inline. Expect `startApifyRun` NOT called.
2. `fetch()` — JSON-LD failure path: mock `safeExternalFetch` to return HTML without JSON-LD or blocked. Expect all-null aggregates returned. Expect `startApifyRun` NOT called (fetch just returns null aggregates; service decides to call startRun).
3. `startRun()` — success: mock `startApifyRun` to return `{ runId: 'r1', defaultDatasetId: 'ds1' }`. Expect adapter returns `{ runId: 'r1', datasetId: 'ds1' }`.
4. `startRun()` — failure: mock `startApifyRun` to return null. Expect adapter returns null.
5. `mapDatasetItems()` — valid items: provide a representative Booking actor output array. Expect correct `rating` and `reviewsCount` extracted.
6. `mapDatasetItems()` — empty array: returns `emptyReputationResult()`.
7. `mapDatasetItems()` — malformed items: does not throw, degrades gracefully.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 7 cases pass.
- `runApifyActor` is never called in any test (assert via spy).

tasks.title: Refactor AccommodationExternalReputationService.refresh() to inline/async split
tasks.description:
Modify `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-external-reputation/accommodation-external-reputation.service.ts`.

Refactor `refresh()` to implement the inline/async split logic:

```
for each enabled listing:
  adapter = getReputationAdapter(platform, creds)
  inlineResult = await adapter.fetch(listing)

  if inlineResult has non-null rating OR adapter.startRun is undefined:
    // Fast path resolved inline (Google, Generic, Booking JSON-LD success)
    upsert(inlineResult, run_status='idle')
    inlineSucceeded.push(platform)

  else:
    // Slow path — enqueue async
    runResult = await adapter.startRun(listing)
    if runResult:
      upsert({ run_status='pending', apify_run_id=runResult.runId,
               apify_dataset_id=runResult.datasetId, run_started_at=now() })
      enqueuedAsync.push(platform)
    else:
      upsert({ fetch_status='error', fetch_message='startRun failed', run_status='idle' })
      inlineFailed.push({ platform, error: 'startRun failed' })

return { inlineSucceeded, enqueuedAsync, inlineFailed }
```

Update the return type of `refresh()` to `{ inlineSucceeded: ExternalPlatformEnum[], enqueuedAsync: ExternalPlatformEnum[], inlineFailed: { platform: ExternalPlatformEnum; error: string }[] }`.

Key file:
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-external-reputation/accommodation-external-reputation.service.ts

Acceptance criteria:
- Rate limit check is still enforced (unchanged).
- All-inline path (Google only) works without any DB pending rows.
- Mixed path (Google inline + Airbnb async) produces correct returned shape.
- `pnpm --filter @repo/service-core typecheck` passes.

subtasks.title: Update refresh() loop to inline/async split
Update return type to { inlineSucceeded, enqueuedAsync, inlineFailed }

tasks.title: Write unit tests for AccommodationExternalReputationService.refresh()
tasks.description:
Create or extend the service test file for `AccommodationExternalReputationService`.

Test cases:
1. All-inline path (Google only): adapter has no `startRun`, fetch returns rating. Expect `inlineSucceeded=['GOOGLE']`, `enqueuedAsync=[]`, `inlineFailed=[]`. No DB pending rows.
2. Mixed path (Google inline + Airbnb async): Google fetch returns rating; Airbnb fetch returns null + has `startRun`. `startRun` returns runResult. Expect `inlineSucceeded=['GOOGLE']`, `enqueuedAsync=['AIRBNB']`.
3. All-async (Airbnb only): expect `inlineSucceeded=[]`, `enqueuedAsync=['AIRBNB']`, pending row written.
4. startRun failure degrades: `startRun` returns null. Expect platform in `inlineFailed` with error message, `fetch_status='error'` upserted, `run_status='idle'`.
5. Rate limit still enforced: second call within window returns 429-equivalent error.
6. Booking JSON-LD success → inline path (no startRun called).
7. Booking JSON-LD miss → async path (startRun called).

All external HTTP calls and DB operations are mocked.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 7 cases pass.
- Coverage on `refresh()` >= 90%.

2. If `enqueuedAsync.length > 0`: return HTTP 202 with body `{ success: true, data: { inlineSucceeded, enqueuedAsync, inlineFailed } }`.
3. If `enqueuedAsync.length === 0`: return HTTP 200 with body `{ success: true, data: { succeeded: inlineSucceeded, failed: inlineFailed } }` (preserving backward-compat shape for all-inline case).

tasks.title: Create GET reputation-status route (protected/reputation-status.ts)
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/routes/accommodation-external-reputation/protected/reputation-status.ts`.

Endpoint: `GET /api/v1/protected/accommodations/:id/external-reputation/status`

Behavior:
1. Auth: `createProtectedRoute`, same ownership enforcement as the refresh route (actor must own the accommodation OR have `ACCOMMODATION_UPDATE_ANY` permission).
2. Read all `accommodation_external_reputation` rows for the given accommodation ID from the model (no Apify calls).
3. Build response:
```ts
{
  success: true,
  data: {
    platforms: {
      [platform]: {
        runStatus: 'idle' | 'pending' | 'running',
        fetchStatus: 'ok' | 'error' | 'blocked' | 'not_found',
        rating: number | null,
        reviewsCount: number | null,
        aggregateFetchedAt: string | null  // ISO 8601
      }
    },
    allSettled: boolean  // true when every platform has runStatus='idle'
  }
}
```
4. Errors: 403 if not owner, 404 if accommodation not found.

Key file:
- apps/api/src/routes/accommodation-external-reputation/protected/reputation-status.ts (new)

Acceptance criteria:
- Route is typed and uses `ResponseFactory` for responses.
- Does NOT trigger any Apify HTTP calls.
- `pnpm --filter apps/api typecheck` passes.

tasks.title: Write integration tests for refresh (202/200/429/403) and status routes
tasks.description:
Create or extend `apps/api/test/.../accommodation-external-reputation.test.ts`.

Test cases:

Refresh endpoint (`POST /:id/external-reputation/refresh`):
1. Owner triggers refresh with Apify-backed platforms → 202 returned, response body has `enqueuedAsync` non-empty.
2. Owner triggers refresh with Google-only accommodation → 200 returned, `succeeded` non-empty.
3. Second refresh within rate-limit window → 429 with `Retry-After` header.
4. Non-owner attempts refresh → 403.
5. Refresh on non-existent accommodation → 404.

Status endpoint (`GET /:id/external-reputation/status`):
6. Owner reads status when all platforms idle → 200, `allSettled: true`.
7. Owner reads status when Apify platform pending → 200, `allSettled: false`, that platform's `runStatus='pending'`.
8. Non-owner reads status → 403.
9. Status on non-existent accommodation → 404.

Mock service layer and DB; use existing route test patterns from the project.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 9 test cases pass.
- `pnpm --filter apps/api test` passes.

tasks.title: Create poll-apify-reputation-runs.job.ts cron handler
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/cron/jobs/poll-apify-reputation-runs.job.ts`.

Implement the cron handler following the pseudocode in the spec's Technical section:
- Read `HOSPEDA_EXTREP_APIFY_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS` from env (default 600_000).
- Call `getReputationAdapterCredentials()` from `apps/api/src/utils/reputation-credentials.ts` at the start of each tick (not cached).
- Call `reputationModel.findPendingRuns()` to get all pending/running rows.
- For each row: call `getApifyRunStatus()`; branch on status:
  - SUCCEEDED: call `getApifyDatasetItems()` → `adapter.mapDatasetItems()` → `upsertReputation()` with `fetchStatus='ok'`, `runStatus='idle'`, cleared run IDs.
  - FAILED / ABORTED / TIMED-OUT: `upsertReputation()` with `fetchStatus='error'`, `runStatus='idle'`, cleared run IDs.
  - READY / RUNNING within timeout: `updateRunStatus(row.id, 'running')` if not already 'running'.
  - READY / RUNNING past timeout: `upsertReputation()` with `fetchStatus='error'`, `fetchMessage='Apify run timed out'`, `runStatus='idle'`, cleared run IDs.
- Return `{ success, message, processed, errors, durationMs }`.
- Zero pending rows → return immediately with `processed: 0, errors: 0`.
- Each row's error is caught individually; one failure does not abort the loop.

Key file:
- apps/api/src/cron/jobs/poll-apify-reputation-runs.job.ts (new)

Acceptance criteria:
- Handler is typed; does not import from `runApifyActor`.
- `pnpm --filter apps/api typecheck` passes.

tasks.title: Write unit tests for poll-apify-reputation-runs cron job
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/cron/jobs/test/poll-apify-reputation-runs.job.test.ts` (or matching test directory).

Test cases:
1. SUCCEEDED path: mock `findPendingRuns` returns 1 row; `getApifyRunStatus` returns `{ status: 'SUCCEEDED', defaultDatasetId: 'ds1' }`; `getApifyDatasetItems` returns items; adapter `mapDatasetItems` returns a result. Expect `upsertReputation` called with `fetchStatus='ok'`, `runStatus='idle'`, `apifyRunId=null`. Expect `processed=1, errors=0`.
2. FAILED path: `getApifyRunStatus` returns `{ status: 'FAILED' }`. Expect `upsertReputation` called with `fetchStatus='error'`, `runStatus='idle'`. Expect `errors=1`.
3. TIMED-OUT path: `getApifyRunStatus` returns `{ status: 'TIMED-OUT' }`. Same expectations as FAILED.
4. ABORTED path: `getApifyRunStatus` returns `{ status: 'ABORTED' }`. Same as FAILED.
5. RUNNING within timeout: `runStartedAt` is 1 min ago, `getApifyRunStatus` returns `{ status: 'RUNNING' }`. Expect `updateRunStatus` called with `'running'`. No upsertReputation for result.
6. Timeout sweep: `runStartedAt` is 20 min ago (> default 10 min), Apify still `RUNNING`. Expect `upsertReputation` with `fetchMessage='Apify run timed out'`, `runStatus='idle'`.
7. Zero pending rows noop: `findPendingRuns` returns `[]`. Expect no Apify calls, `processed=0, errors=0`, exits cleanly.
8. Apify API unreachable: `getApifyRunStatus` returns null. Expect row skipped (no upsert), logged warning, loop continues.
9. Per-row error isolation: first row throws, second row processes successfully. Expect `errors=1, processed=1`.

All mocks via vitest `vi.fn()` / `vi.mock()`.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 9 cases pass.
- Coverage on the cron handler >= 90%.

tasks.title: Create useReputationStatus polling hook (apps/web/src/hooks/use-reputation-status.ts)
Modify SPEC-237 owner reputation panel to use useReputationStatus and render per-platform status chips
tasks.description:
Locate the existing SPEC-237 owner reputation panel component:
```bash
grep -rn "external-reputation\|externalReputation\|ExternalReputation" apps/web/src/ --include="*.astro" --include="*.tsx" -l
```

Modify the component to:
1. Import and call `useReputationStatus(accommodationId, enabled)` — `enabled` is true after the owner clicks refresh.
2. Pass `runStatus` to each platform's display chip.
3. Render per-platform state chips:
   - `runStatus='pending' | 'running'`: spinner chip + i18n key `externalReputation.status.pending` / `.running`, previously-cached rating in muted style (stale indicator).
   - `runStatus='idle' && fetchStatus='ok'`: show rating badge normally.
   - `runStatus='idle' && fetchStatus='error'|'blocked'|'not_found'`: show error badge with `fetchMessage` as tooltip + i18n error key.
4. After clicking refresh and receiving a 202: set `enabled=true` to start polling.
5. After receiving a 200 (all inline): do NOT start polling.
6. All status chip text via `@repo/i18n` (keys added in T-029).
7. `aria-live="polite"` on the status chip container.
8. Spinner animation wrapped in CSS `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` suppression.

Key files:
- apps/web/src/components/.../<RepeatationPanelComponent>.tsx (locate via grep)

Acceptance criteria:
- No Tailwind classes (vanilla CSS / CSS modules only).
- All strings via i18n keys.
- `pnpm --filter apps/web typecheck` passes.

Set enabled=true on 202 response, false on 200

tasks.title: Write unit tests for useReputationStatus hook
tasks.description:
Create `apps/web/src/hooks/test/use-reputation-status.test.ts` (or `.tsx`).

Test cases using vitest + `@testing-library/react` (or the project's existing hook testing pattern):

1. Does NOT poll when `enabled=false`.
2. Polls immediately when `enabled=true` (mocked `fetch` called on mount).
3. Repeats polling every 10 s (advance fake timers by 10 s, assert fetch called again).
4. Stops polling when `allSettled=true` is returned from the status endpoint.
5. On 4xx response: sets `error` string, stops polling.
6. On 5xx response: sets `error` string, stops polling.
7. On network error: does NOT set error (transient), continues polling.
8. Cleanup: `setInterval` is cleared on unmount (no memory leak).

All tests mock `fetch` via `vi.fn()`; use `vi.useFakeTimers()` for timer control.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 8 cases pass.
- No real HTTP calls in tests.

tasks.title: Write regression test: weekly cron enqueues via startRun, not runApifyActor
tasks.description:
Create a regression test that verifies the existing weekly `refresh-external-reputation` cron job (from SPEC-237) now calls `startRun()` for Apify-backed platforms instead of `runApifyActor()`.

Test location: `apps/api/src/cron/jobs/test/refresh-external-reputation.job.regression.test.ts` (or extend the existing test for that job).

Test case:
- Mock `runApifyActor` with a spy.
- Mock `startApifyRun` with a spy returning a valid run result.
- Trigger the weekly cron handler for an accommodation that has an Airbnb listing.
- Assert: `runApifyActor` spy was NEVER called.
- Assert: `startApifyRun` spy WAS called for the Airbnb platform.
- Assert: DB row for Airbnb has `run_status='pending'`, `apify_run_id` set.

This guards against future regressions where the sync endpoint is accidentally restored.

Acceptance criteria:
- Test passes.
- `runApifyActor` call count in the test = 0.
- `startApifyRun` call count >= 1.

tasks.title: Run full pnpm test + typecheck + lint suite and fix any regressions
tasks.description:
Run the full quality suite across all affected packages and fix any issues found:

1. `pnpm typecheck` — fix any TypeScript errors in @repo/db, @repo/schemas, @repo/service-core, apps/api, apps/web.
2. `pnpm lint` — fix any Biome lint errors. Pay attention to:
   - `useDefaultParameterLast` (params with defaults after required)
   - `noExplicitAny` (use proper types)
   - `useExhaustiveDependencies` (React hooks deps)
   - `noUnusedVariables` (prefix with `_`)
3. `pnpm test` — run full test suite, fix any broken tests. Common failure points:
   - Schema parse tests if new required fields were accidentally added.
   - Mock-based tests that assert old method signatures.
   - The schedules-manifest sync test (should be green from T-028).

Key scopes to check:
- `pnpm --filter @repo/db typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @repo/schemas typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @repo/service-core test`
- `pnpm --filter apps/api test`
- `pnpm --filter apps/web test`

Acceptance criteria:

- `pnpm lint` exits 0.
- `pnpm test` exits 0 with no failing tests.

tasks.title: Note owner ops required: set HOSPEDA_EXTREP_POLL_SCHEDULE + HOSPEDA_EXTREP_APIFY_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS in Coolify
tasks.description:
Document the operational requirements introduced by the two new env vars added in T-004.

Update `apps/api/.env.example` (if not already done in T-004) with comments explaining each var:
- `HOSPEDA_EXTREP_POLL_SCHEDULE` — cron expression, default `*/2 * * * *`.
- `HOSPEDA_EXTREP_APIFY_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS` — milliseconds before a stuck run is swept, default `600000`.

Create (or update) a note in the PR description / progress.md reminding the owner:
1. Set `HOSPEDA_EXTREP_POLL_SCHEDULE` in Coolify for `hospeda-api-staging` and `hospeda-api-prod` (the defaults are fine for most deployments — only set if custom scheduling is needed).
2. Set `HOSPEDA_EXTREP_APIFY_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS` in Coolify if the default 10 min timeout needs adjustment.
3. The existing `HOSPEDA_APIFY_TOKEN` (from SPEC-237) must already be set — without it, `startRun()` returns null and all Apify platforms degrade to `fetch_status='error'`.

Key file:
- apps/api/.env.example (update comments)
- .qtm/tasks/SPEC-250-external-reputation-async-refresh/progress.md (new note, or add to this file)

Acceptance criteria:
- `.env.example` has both vars with descriptive comments.
- Ops note is documented somewhere visible (progress.md or PR template notes).
